texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: OPERATOR RANGES AND SPACEABILITY: EXTENDING A RESULT OF KITSON AND TIMONEY
texts.text: OPERATOR RANGES AND SPACEABILITY: EXTENDING A RESULT OF KITSON AND TIMONEY

texts.label: text
texts.orig: GEIVISON RIBEIRO
texts.text: GEIVISON RIBEIRO

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Abstract. We revisit the results of Kitson and Timoney [J. Math. Anal. Appl. 378 (2011), 680686] on the spaceability of complements of operator ranges, extending one of their main theorems to the general Fr´ echet setting. In particular, we provide an affirmative answer to the question posed in Remark 3.4 of that paper, showing that the conclusion remains valid when the operators act between Fr´ echet spaces. Moreover, we show that the same phenomenon occurs for arbitrary (possibly uncountable) families of operators. The arguments presented here follow the spirit of the original work.
texts.text: Abstract. We revisit the results of Kitson and Timoney [J. Math. Anal. Appl. 378 (2011), 680686] on the spaceability of complements of operator ranges, extending one of their main theorems to the general Fr´ echet setting. In particular, we provide an affirmative answer to the question posed in Remark 3.4 of that paper, showing that the conclusion remains valid when the operators act between Fr´ echet spaces. Moreover, we show that the same phenomenon occurs for arbitrary (possibly uncountable) families of operators. The arguments presented here follow the spirit of the original work.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 1. Introduction
texts.text: 1. Introduction

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The notions of lineability and spaceability trace back to the pioneering ideas of Gurariy [13] and were later formalized, in their modern form, by Aron, Gurariy and Seoane-Sep´ ulveda in 2005 [4]. Since then, these topics have become an active field of research in Analysis (and particularly in Functional Analysis), with a systematic treatment given in the 2016 monograph by Aron, BernalGonz´ alez, Pellegrino and Seoane-Sep´ ulveda [5]. Over the past decades, several general criteria and refinements have been established; see, among others, Bernal-Gonz´ alez and Ord´ o˜ nez-Cabrera (2014) [6], F´ avaro, Pellegrino and Tomaz (2020) [11], and Calder´ on-Moreno, Gerlach-Mena and Prado-Bassas (2023) [8], as well as more recent contributions in [1, 2, 3, 16, 12, 9, 18, 15].
texts.text: The notions of lineability and spaceability trace back to the pioneering ideas of Gurariy [13] and were later formalized, in their modern form, by Aron, Gurariy and Seoane-Sep´ ulveda in 2005 [4]. Since then, these topics have become an active field of research in Analysis (and particularly in Functional Analysis), with a systematic treatment given in the 2016 monograph by Aron, BernalGonz´ alez, Pellegrino and Seoane-Sep´ ulveda [5]. Over the past decades, several general criteria and refinements have been established; see, among others, Bernal-Gonz´ alez and Ord´ o˜ nez-Cabrera (2014) [6], F´ avaro, Pellegrino and Tomaz (2020) [11], and Calder´ on-Moreno, Gerlach-Mena and Prado-Bassas (2023) [8], as well as more recent contributions in [1, 2, 3, 16, 12, 9, 18, 15].

texts.label: text
texts.orig: A conceptual milestone in this theory is due to Drewnowski (1984) [10]: if T : E → F is a continuous linear operator between Fr´ echet spaces with non-closed range, then the complement ( F \ T ( E )) ∪{ 0 } contains a closed infinite-dimensional subspace. This result has become a central tool in the study of large linear structures within nonlinear sets.
texts.text: A conceptual milestone in this theory is due to Drewnowski (1984) [10]: if T : E → F is a continuous linear operator between Fr´ echet spaces with non-closed range, then the complement ( F \ T ( E )) ∪{ 0 } contains a closed infinite-dimensional subspace. This result has become a central tool in the study of large linear structures within nonlinear sets.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Following this perspective, Kitson and Timoney (2011) [14] proved that, for a Fr´ echet space X and a countable family of continuous linear operators ( T n : Z n → X ) n ∈ N (with each Z n being a Banach space), the complement of the linear span of the ranges is spaceable whenever that span is not closed. The authors also asked whether their result extends to the case in which the domain spaces Z n are themselves Fr´ echet (see Remark 3.4 in [14]).
texts.text: Following this perspective, Kitson and Timoney (2011) [14] proved that, for a Fr´ echet space X and a countable family of continuous linear operators ( T n : Z n → X ) n ∈ N (with each Z n being a Banach space), the complement of the linear span of the ranges is spaceable whenever that span is not closed. The authors also asked whether their result extends to the case in which the domain spaces Z n are themselves Fr´ echet (see Remark 3.4 in [14]).

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The present paper gives an affirmative answer to this question. We show that the same result remains valid for families of operators T n : Z n → X acting between Fr´ echet spaces, by encoding the family into the range of a single operator and applying geometric arguments in the spirit of [10]. Moreover, we remove the countability assumption and show that the same conclusion holds for arbitrary (possibly uncountable) families of operators.
texts.text: The present paper gives an affirmative answer to this question. We show that the same result remains valid for families of operators T n : Z n → X acting between Fr´ echet spaces, by encoding the family into the range of a single operator and applying geometric arguments in the spirit of [10]. Moreover, we remove the countability assumption and show that the same conclusion holds for arbitrary (possibly uncountable) families of operators.

texts.text: In this section we collect auxiliary notions and tools that will be used throughout the paper.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Definition 2.1. [7, Definition 8.1.6] Let E be a topological vector space over K ∈ { R , C } . A set B ⊂ E is said to be balanced if λx ∈ B for every x ∈ B and every λ ∈ K with | λ | ≤ 1 .
texts.text: Definition 2.1. [7, Definition 8.1.6] Let E be a topological vector space over K ∈ { R , C } . A set B ⊂ E is said to be balanced if λx ∈ B for every x ∈ B and every λ ∈ K with | λ | ≤ 1 .

texts.orig: We now recall a fundamental geometric result underlying our approach.
texts.text: We now recall a fundamental geometric result underlying our approach.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Theorem 2.4 ([14, Prop. 2.4]; see also [10]) . Let E and F be Fr´ echet spaces, and let T : E → F be a continuous linear operator. If T ( E ) is not closed in F , then the complement F \ T ( E ) contains a closed infinite-dimensional subspace.
texts.text: Theorem 2.4 ([14, Prop. 2.4]; see also [10]) . Let E and F be Fr´ echet spaces, and let T : E → F be a continuous linear operator. If T ( E ) is not closed in F , then the complement F \ T ( E ) contains a closed infinite-dimensional subspace.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: In fact, this geometric statement was originally proved by Drewnowski [10], under the assumption that T ( E ) is not relatively open or not barrelled. Here we follow the presentation adopted by Kitson and Timoney [14], who used this result as a central tool in their study of spaceable complements of operator ranges.
texts.text: In fact, this geometric statement was originally proved by Drewnowski [10], under the assumption that T ( E ) is not relatively open or not barrelled. Here we follow the presentation adopted by Kitson and Timoney [14], who used this result as a central tool in their study of spaceable complements of operator ranges.

Since these vectors have disjoint supports, they are linearly independent, and therefore dim E = ∞ .

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The statement of Theorem 3.1 was given for countable families ( T n : Z n → X ) ∞ n =1 . We now show that the same conclusion remains valid for uncountable families of continuous linear operators.
texts.text: The statement of Theorem 3.1 was given for countable families ( T n : Z n → X ) ∞ n =1 . We now show that the same conclusion remains valid for uncountable families of continuous linear operators.

texts.text: Since ˜ X is closed in X , the same F is closed in X . Finally, because ˜ Y 0 = Y 0 ∩ ˜ X ⊂ Y ∩ ˜ X , we have

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Therefore X \ Y contains the closed infinite-dimensional subspace F \ { 0 } and is spaceable, as claimed. □
